title: Ctenitis subglandulosa
intro: Ctenitis subglandulosa, also known as a type of fern, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia. The health benefits of Ctenitis subglandulosa include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ctenitis subglandulosa is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
uses: Ctenitis subglandulosa, a plant native to certain regions, has been utilized in traditional medicine for various purposes.
